mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8.0% and continues to increase, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100 and RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the partial correlation of the interaction term with forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained decline in housing starts reduces construction activity and consumer wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not systematically decline when housing starts drop during periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with payroll growth exceed 100k are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.5% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation readings.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth slows below 2.0% annualized while payroll employment continues to rise above 150k monthly indicates a productivity-driven earnings decoupling that ultimately pressures corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% and PAYEMS monthly change > 150k
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the partial correlation of the interaction term with forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, a sustained increase in industrial production signals resilient supply capacity that offsets demand weakness, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not systematically rise when industrial production increases during periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%.
